data.snippet: For more than a year, Elon Musk has repeatedly said Tesla is months away from launching a driverless robotaxi service in California – once state regulators give their blessing.

image.text: A Tesla robotaxi drives on the street along South Congress Avenue in Austin, Texas, U.S., June 22, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Tesla and German union IG Metall have agreed for now to set aside their dispute over a labour meeting at the carmaker's plant outside Berlin this month, the union said on Thursday. Tesla had filed a criminal complaint against a member of the union it accused of secretly recording the meeting on February 10. IG Metall described the allegation as a "calculated lie".
relatedHeadlines.text: German union says truce reached in dispute with Tesla

relatedHeadlines.snippet:
Tesla CEO Elon Musk said on
Thursday that the company has started Robotaxi rides in Austin
without safety monitors in the car.

 (Reporting by Akash Sriram in Bengaluru; Editing by Alan
Barona)
 ((Akash.Sriram@thomsonreuters.com; On X as @HoodieOnVeshti;
+91-99017-77617;))

nL4N3YN1U4
relatedHeadlines.text: Tesla starts robotaxi rides without safety monitors in Austin

data.text: Musk touts California robotaxis but Tesla does nothing to get permits

data.snippet: Walmart has agreed to pay $100 million to settle charges that the company caused delivery drivers to lose tens of millions of dollars in earnings, the U.S. Federal Trade Commission said in a statement on Thursday.

image.text: A view shows the logo of a Walmart store in Ciudad Juarez, Mexico June 24, 2024.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Amazon on Thursday lost its latest bid to try and throw out two mass lawsuits from retailers and consumers worth up to 4 billion pounds ($5.41 billion) for allegedly abusing its dominant position.
relatedHeadlines.text: Amazon refused permission to appeal go-ahead for UK lawsuits from retailers, consumers

data.text: Walmart agrees to pay $100 million to settle deceptive earnings charges, FTC says

data.snippet: The Trump administration has settled on a plan that would require big oil refineries to make up for at least half of the biofuel blending volumes obligations waived in recent years under the Small Refinery Exemption program, according to three sources familiar with the discussions.

image.text: Ethanol fuel is shown being pumped into a vehicle at a gas station selling alternative fuels in the town of Nevada, Iowa, December 6, 2007.

data.text: EXCLUSIVE-Trump EPA to shift at least half of waived biofuel obligations to big refiners, sources say

data.snippet: A Texas judge on Thursday rejected Kenvue's bid to dismiss a lawsuit by state Attorney General Ken Paxton accusing the company of failing to warn consumers that it may be unsafe for pregnant women to take Tylenol.

image.text: The company logo for Kenvue Inc. Johnson & Johnson's consumer-health business, is displayed on during the company's IPO at the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) in New York City, U.S., May 4, 2023.

data.text: Judge rejects Kenvue bid to dismiss Texas lawsuit over Tylenol safety

data.snippet: The U.S. Treasury handed Swiss private bank MBaer Merchant Bank AG a potentially crippling blow on Thursday by threatening to sever its access to the U.S. financial system on the grounds it had breached sanctions against Iran, Russia and Venezuela.

image.text: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent attends a Senate Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs Committee hearing on the Financial Stability Oversight Council's annual report to Congress, on Capitol Hill in Washington, D.C., U.S., February 5, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Fund manager Vanguard Group said on Thursday it agreed to settle litigation filed by the Republican attorneys general of Texas, Kansas and other states, who had alleged the fund manager and rivals violated antitrust law through their climate activism.
relatedHeadlines.text: Vanguard settles Republican states' antitrust suit for $29.5 million

data.text: US threatens to cut off Swiss bank MBaer from financial system over alleged Iran, Russia, Venezuela ties

data.snippet: Instagram said it would notify parents if their teenager repeatedly searches for terms related to suicide or self-harm within a short period, as pressure grows for governments to follow Australia's ban on the use of social media for under 16s.

image.text: Instagram app icon is seen on a smartphone in this illustration taken October 27, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: The former psychotherapist of a California woman suing Meta's Instagram and Alphabet's YouTube, claiming a childhood addiction to the platforms damaged her mental health, testified on Tuesday that adolescent social media usage was a contributing factor.
relatedHeadlines.text: Social media addiction trial takes new turn with therapist's testimony

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Australia in December became the world's first country to ban social media for children under 16, blocking them from platforms including TikTok, Alphabet's YouTube and Meta's Instagram and Facebook.
relatedHeadlines.text: FACTBOX-From Australia to Europe, countries move to curb children's social media access

data.text: Instagram to alert parents on teen suicide searches as UK weighs social media ban

data.snippet: Years of court rulings against multinational food delivery firms in Italy have so far failed to curb their alleged exploitation of workers, according to the workers themselves and the lawyers who represent them.

image.text: A delivery worker with a backpack of Deliveroo rides a bike in central town in Rome, Italy, February 25, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Milan prosecutors have placed the Italian arm of food delivery platform Deliveroo under judicial supervision and its chief executive under investigation for alleged exploitation of workers, judicial documents seen by Reuters showed on Wednesday.
relatedHeadlines.text: Deliveroo's Italian arm placed under supervision over alleged labour exploitation

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Milan prosecutors have placed the Italian arm of Spanish food delivery platform Glovo under judicial supervision and its chief executive under investigation for alleged exploitation of workers, judicial documents seen by Reuters showed on Monday.
relatedHeadlines.text: Glovo's Italian food delivery arm placed under supervision over alleged labour exploitation

data.text: Italy's delivery riders keep winning in court but losing on the streets

data.snippet: Apple urged a federal judge to dismiss a proposed class action claiming it defrauded shareholders twice, by overstating the artificial intelligence capabilities of its Siri voice assistant and falsely representing its compliance with an injunction governing commissions on app sales.

image.text: An Apple logo hangs above the entrance to the Apple store on 5th Avenue in the Manhattan borough of New York City, July 21, 2015.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: An adviser to Europe's top court on Thursday sided with EU regulators demanding information regarding two antitrust investigations of Meta Platforms, in a setback for the U.S.-based social media company.
relatedHeadlines.text: Setback for Meta as court adviser backs EU regulators in data spat

data.text: Apple seeks dismissal of fraud lawsuit over Siri AI, Epic injunction

data.snippet: Norway's $2.2 trillion sovereign wealth fund, the world's largest, is using AI to screen companies for risks such as potential links to forced labour and corruption, and help avoid financial losses as a result, it said on Thursday.
data.text: Norway's wealth fund using AI to screen for ESG risks

data.snippet: The continent of Africa looks set to emerge as a key driver of global solar power production over the rest of the 2020s thanks to a potent mix of policy support, rapid economic growth and declining component costs.

image.text: A view shows solar panels at the green hydrogen proof-of-concept site in Vredendal, Western Cape, South Africa, November 15, 2022.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: While North America and Europe scramble to build the data centers needed to run 21st-century businesses, many emerging markets remain anchored to 20th-century products like steel, cement and plastics for job creation and economic growth.
relatedHeadlines.text: ROI-Old-economy demands may stall emerging market decarbonization: Maguire

data.text: ROI-Africa primed for solar breakthrough after record capacity growth: Maguire

data.snippet: Fiji and Tuvalu will host key meetings in the lead-up to the COP31 climate summit in 2026, the Pacific Islands Forum said on Thursday.
data.text: Fiji and Tuvalu to host pre-COP31 climate meetings

data.snippet: Poland's antimonopoly office UOKiK said on Thursday it had searched the headquarters of e-commerce platform Allegro in Poznan and its offices in Warsaw as part of explanatory proceedings.

image.text: Packages move through the Polish marketplace Allegro (ALE.WA) distribution centre, ahead of Christmas, in Adamow, Poland, December 11, 2024.

data.text: Poland's competition watchdog searches Allegro offices in Poznan, Warsaw

data.snippet: The criminal group behind a hack at Dutch telecom Odido, one of the largest carried out in the Netherlands, has begun publishing customer information on the dark web, the company said on Thursday.

image.text: Figurines with computers and smartphones are seen in front of the words "Cyber Attack" in this illustration taken, February 19, 2024.

data.text: Hacking group begins leaking customer data in Dutch telecom Odido hack

image.text: The eBay logo is displayed at the eBay booth during CES 2026, an annual consumer electronics trade show, in Las Vegas, Nevada, U.S., January 6, 2026.

data.text: Ebay slashes 6% of workforce as e-commerce firm realigns operations

data.snippet: Air France KLM, British Airways and their peers on Thursday lost their fight against EU cartel fines totalling 776 million euros ($916 million), ending a more than two-decade old saga.

image.text: The logo of Air France-KLM Group is seen during the Air France-KLM Group's Annual Results 2025 press conference in Paris, France, February 19, 2026.

data.text: EU top court rejects Air France, others' fight against EU cargo cartel fine
